items.guide_quote: 2 months up to 6 months 1/2 tablet Once daily 10 days / 5 tablets
items.description: Zinc dosage for children 2 months up to 6 months: half tablet once daily for 10 days (supply 5 tablets).

items.guide_quote: 6 months up to 5 years 1 tablet Once daily 10 days / 10 tablets
items.description: Zinc dosage for children 6 months up to 5 years: 1 tablet once daily for 10 days (supply 10 tablets).

items.guide_quote: Dissolve the zinc tablet (or half tablet) in breast milk or clean water on a spoon. Give the full 10 days even if diarrhoea stops.
items.description: Zinc administration instructions: dissolve in breast milk or clean water, give full 10-day course even if diarrhoea stops.

items.guide_quote: 1. Wash hands with soap and water. 2. Pour entire ORS packet into a clean container. 3. Add 1 litre of clean water and mix until dissolved.
items.description: ORS preparation steps: wash hands, pour packet into clean container, add 1 litre clean water, mix until dissolved.

items.guide_quote: If the child vomits, wait 10 minutes, then give more slowly.
items.description: ORS administration guidance: if child vomits, wait 10 minutes then give more slowly.

items.guide_quote: Store mixed ORS in a covered container; discard after 24 hours.
items.description: Mixed ORS solution must be discarded after 24 hours.

items.guide_quote: 1. Check expiry date; do not use expired tests. 2. Wear new gloves for each child. 3. Clean the child's ring finger with a spirit swab; let dry. 4. Prick the finger with a lancet; collect blood with the loop. 5. Place blood in square hole A on the test strip. 6. Add 6 drops of buffer into round hole B. 7. Wait 15 minutes. 8. Read results:
items.description: Complete RDT procedure for malaria testing including 8 steps from expiry check to reading results.

items.guide_quote: POSITIVE = red lines in both C and T windows.
items.description: RDT result is positive when red lines appear in both C and T windows.

items.guide_quote: NEGATIVE = red line in C window only.
items.description: RDT result is negative when a red line appears in C window only.

items.guide_quote: INVALID = no line in C window; repeat with new test.
items.description: RDT result is invalid when no line appears in C window; must repeat with a new test.

items.guide_quote: assess, classify, and treat sick children age 2 months up to 5 years
items.description: Target population for community case management: children aged 2 months up to 5 years.

items.guide_quote: Identify fever by the caregiver's report or by feeling the child's stomach or underarm.
items.description: Fever can be identified by caregiver report or by feeling the child's stomach or underarm.

items.guide_quote: Count the child's breaths for one full minute while the child is calm and quiet.
items.description: Respiratory rate must be counted for one full minute while child is calm and quiet.

items.guide_quote: The child must be calm; do not assess while crying or breastfeeding.
items.description: Chest indrawing assessment requires the child to be calm; do not assess while crying or breastfeeding.

items.guide_quote: Give at least half a cup after each loose stool.
items.description: Give at least half a cup of ORS after each loose stool.

items.guide_quote: Do not give antimalarial medicine unless the RDT is positive.
items.description: RDT positive result is required before giving antimalarial medicine.

items.guide_quote: If still fast breathing, refer.
items.description: Fast breathing detected on breath count assessment indicates need for referral.

items.description: Fever persisting at 3-day follow-up is a referral criterion.

items.guide_quote: complete the full 5-day course of amoxicillin
items.description: Treatment protocol requiring completion of a full 5-day course of amoxicillin when breathing is normal and child is improving at follow-up.

items.guide_quote: 2 months up to 3 years 1 tablet AL Twice daily 3 days / 6 tablets
items.description: AL dosage for children aged 2 months up to 3 years: 1 tablet twice daily for 3 days (6 tablets total).

items.guide_quote: 3 years up to 5 years 2 tablets AL Twice daily 3 days /12 tablets
items.description: AL dosage for children aged 3 years up to 5 years: 2 tablets twice daily for 3 days (12 tablets total).

items.guide_quote: Give first dose immediately. Second dose 8 hours later. Then morning and evening for 2 more days.
items.description: AL administration schedule: first dose immediately, second dose 8 hours later, then twice daily for 2 more days.

items.guide_quote: Crush tablet and mix with breast milk, water, or food.
items.description: Instruction to crush AL tablet and mix with breast milk, water, or food for administration to young children.

items.guide_quote: If the child spits out the entire dose, give another.
items.description: If the child spits out the entire dose, another dose should be given.

items.guide_quote: If RDT is negative: do not give antimalarial. Follow up in 3 days.
items.description: When RDT is negative, antimalarial should not be given and child should be followed up in 3 days.

items.guide_quote: When referring a child: 1. Explain to the caregiver why the child must go to the health facility.
items.description: Module covering the 7-step referral procedure for referring a sick child to a health facility.

items.guide_quote: If fever, keep the child cool during travel.
items.description: During referral travel, a febrile child should be kept cool.

items.guide_quote: If diarrhoea, give ORS solution during travel.
items.description: During referral travel, a child with diarrhoea should be given ORS solution.

items.guide_quote: Follow up the child after return, at least once a week until well.
items.description: Post-referral follow-up requirement: at least once a week until the child is well.

items.guide_quote: Do a Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT) for malaria before treating. Do not give antimalarial medicine unless the RDT is positive.
items.description: Requirement to perform RDT before treating fever in a malaria area; antimalarial only if positive.

items.description: AL (Artemether-Lumefantrine) tablets as a required CHW supply item for malaria treatment.

items.guide_quote: When referring a child: 1. Explain to the caregiver why the child must go to the health facility. 2.
